content.boundary: Applies to villages under 2,000 inhabitants where enrollment has fallen below the national minimum class-size threshold or reached zero (e.g., Garab-type cases); does not apply where a viable networked or grade-split alternative under T2/T3 remains feasible and unexplored.
content.change_lever: Legislate a formal closure procedure requiring documented alternatives assessment, statutory free transport guarantees, targeted compensatory support (TEIP-style) for displaced disadvantaged pupils, and mandatory consultation with affected communities before any closure decision becomes final.
content.name: Consolidation with statutory mitigation: closure as managed default plus transport, equity and consultation guarantees
content.system_problem: Village school closures currently occur through discretionary district-center decisions ('temporary closure'/'suspension') without a dedicated statutory procedure, without clarified transport-obligation assignment, and without consistent compensatory measures, producing arbitrary variation in how closure harms are distributed and unresolved legal ambiguity over who must provide transport.
